short_description: A beginner-friendly Simple RAG (Red, Amber, Green) project for Human Resources Escalation Triage
domain_summary: Human Resources Escalation Triage involves identifying and addressing employee concerns, resolving conflicts, and escalating issues to higher management when necessary.
user_persona: HR Manager: Responsible for managing employee concerns, resolving conflicts, and escalating issues to higher management when necessary.
architecture_notes: Use a microservices architecture to improve scalability and maintainability
Implement a caching layer to improve performance
Use a message queue to handle asynchronous tasks
testing_strategy: Unit testing, Integration testing, and End-to-end testing
starter_questions: What are the common employee concerns?
How can we improve conflict resolution?
What are the key factors affecting employee satisfaction?
business_rules: Employee concerns must be addressed within 24 hours
Conflicts must be resolved within 48 hours
Employee satisfaction must be monitored regularly

sample_documents.content: Please provide feedback on your experience with our HR department.
CREATE TABLE employees (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), email VARCHAR(255));

tool_catalog.name: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
tool_catalog.description: Used for text analysis and sentiment analysis

tool_catalog.name: Knowledge Graph
tool_catalog.description: Used for storing and querying employee data

tool_catalog.name: Database
tool_catalog.description: Used for storing employee data and feedback

tool_catalog.name: API Gateway
tool_catalog.description: Used for API calls and communication between modules

agent_plan.name: Architecture Agent
agent_plan.role: Define app boundaries, data flow, runtime stack, and integration points.
outputs: Use a microservices architecture to improve scalability and maintainability
Implement a caching layer to improve performance
Use a message queue to handle asynchronous tasks

agent_plan.name: Backend Agent
agent_plan.role: Design FastAPI modules, service contracts, validation, and error handling.

agent_plan.name: Frontend Agent
agent_plan.role: Design React screens, state flow, controls, and user feedback states.

agent_plan.name: Database Agent
agent_plan.role: Design persistence models, sample data, indexes, and audit records.

agent_plan.name: Testing Agent
agent_plan.role: Define contract tests, smoke tests, and generated project validation.
outputs: Unit testing, Integration testing, and End-to-end testing

agent_plan.name: DevOps Agent
agent_plan.role: Define environment variables, Docker workflow, and repository packaging.

agent_plan.name: Reviewer Agent
agent_plan.role: Review the generated plan for completeness, security, and portfolio quality.

architecture_description: Real Estate Policy Assistant for lease and property policy lookup.
problem_statement: Build a portfolio-ready Simple RAG application for Human Resources Escalation Triage Simple RAG - Beginner Custom 19 that returns explainable answers with sources and timeline evidence.
functional_requirements: Accept a user question or task from the React UI.
Validate request shape and reject empty input.
Execute the Simple RAG workflow with observable timeline steps.
Return answer, cited sources, timeline, and project metadata.
Persist each run for debugging, demos, and interview explanation.
non_functional_requirements: Keep local deterministic fallback behavior so the repo runs without paid API keys.
Avoid logging secrets or raw provider credentials.
Keep API responses stable enough for frontend and tests.
Support Docker-based local deployment and PostgreSQL production storage.
Keep generated documentation accurate enough for portfolio review.

api_endpoints.purpose: Health check for deployment and monitoring.
Run the Simple RAG workflow for a user question.
Return recent stored run logs for review and debugging.

scalability_plan: Move long runs to a queue
Use managed vector DB when data grows
